note: 2026-07-08 evening (cycle 6.evening): Server was found UP at slot start (correct, standing rule intact) and stopped cleanly via the canonical WINDOWTITLE kill. AUDIT of session 2026-07-08T00-02-39 (an interactive, non-scheduled session had run since 00:02 with two just-shipped Phase F bug fixes -- see below): 2.5 minutes into that session (04:05:11 UTC) frameTick dropped from 990300 to 0 with no server restart (same session_id, sub-second gap) -- traced to a POST /control/reset call, the only other caller of _reset_world besides __init__. Root-caused as a deliberate manual reset (not a bug): the immediately-preceding interactive work had just hand-patched a population crashed to POPULATION_FLOOR (4 survivors, corrupted ages) caused by an aging-rate bug (_tick_lifecycle was applying an extra 10x multiplier -- fixed in 8902465) and a heal_agent zombie bug (dead agents' incapacitated flag could be flipped back to False, letting corpses resume moving/thinking/getting tasked -- fixed in 6e930ca, all 8 corrupted agents in the live world one-time re-patched). Resetting to a clean 12-agent roster with both fixes live is the obviously sensible thing to do after that discovery. Usable soak was therefore only the ~64-real-minute (~42 sim-minute) fresh-world tail, frame 0-74984 -- PROVISIONAL per the <4h rule, no design-level FAIL found. Per-flag: Phase A/B unchanged/healthy (continuous produces, 4 terraform starts incl. 1 scarcity-reflex, honest terraform rejections); Phase C (GOODS_ENABLED) healthy for a 42-min-old world (storage overflow + spoilage fired, 0 ruins/disrepair as expected against the retuned 0.1/tick decay); Phase D (TECH_TREE_ENABLED) tier gate fired correctly live (cart craft rejected at village tier 1, needs Forge) though council/invention stayed dormant (fresh world, not a regression); Phase E (ECONOMY_ENABLED) still no market built, unexercised not broken; Phase F (LIFECYCLE_ENABLED) -- BOTH just-shipped fixes verified holding under direct test for the first time: aging rate confirmed exactly correct (hand-checked all 12 agents' ages against staggered-seed + frames_elapsed/15000, exact match, e.g. Sage 60 + 74984/15000 = 64.98); heal_agent zombie fix showed no regression (2 heals on Sage, 0 deaths this window so full corpse-revival regression test stays open for next soak with an actual death). 0 LM errors/fallbacks/context overflows across 405 decisions. Watch item: Sage at age 64.98, one gate below DEATH_CHANCE_START_AGE (65) -- first natural-death roll under the corrected rate should fire very soon, watch whether succession resolves cleanly. No design-level FAIL found, so proceeded straight to the next batch item. IMPLEMENT: Phase G (knowledge, culture & personality drift, CULTURE_ENABLED) using the pre-staged .cursor/phase-prompts/phase-G.md scope, explicitly scoped to CULTURE_ENABLED in full depth only -- DIPLOMACY_ENABLED (second settlement + inter-village trade) deliberately deferred to a future slot per the phase prompt's own 'separable, ship later if the slot runs long' guidance, not a shortfall. Spawned an implementer subagent: skills-by-practice (gather/craft/build/heal, deterministic gain per success, small yield/output bonus, folded into the existing 'Your skill:' prompt line at zero token cost); teaching via a deterministic keyword check on talk_to_nearby messages (no new action verb); a seed Library structure whose 'unlocks' effect is cosmetic -- the real mechanic is a capped civilization[libraryKnowledge] ring populated on death and passively studied by any living agent standing in that district (a tick-gated backstop, not a new action); a capped village chronicle rendered as one rotating 'Village history: ...' prompt line; meme mutation via exactly one capped/event-driven lm_complete call per spread (8% roll, hard 30-call session ceiling), storing the reworded text as a per-civilization override so the shared MEMES dict is never mutated, with several defensive layers added after live-testing found qwen (thinking-class) leaking reasoning-scaffold/meta-commentary into the raw response; deterministic personality-drift trait clauses (capped at 3) appended on collapse/bereavement/election win-loss, folded into the personality line like Phase F's life-stage prefix. Reviewed commit 4889c09 (diff against 8902465..HEAD, 756 lines across sim_engine.py/server.py) against the two standing invariants -- no silent rejections (teaching/study/mutation failures are all no-ops on an existing action, never a blocked user-facing decision; meme-mutation rejections from the new scaffold-detection layer are silently discarded by design, matching birth's fallback-never-blocks pattern), deterministic escapes (library knowledge ring caps at 12 with weakest-first eviction, chronicle caps at 20, personality drift caps at 3, meme mutation hard-capped at 30/session -- none of these caps block anything downstream, they just turn a feature off, which is the intended design) -- plus back-compat (restore_state setdefaults for chronicle/libraryKnowledge/memeTexts/memeMutations/skillPracticeCount/teachCount and per-agent skills/personalityTraits/lastTeachFrame, library template merged into old projectRegistry) and prompt-token discipline (skills folded into the existing skill line, chronicle is one new template slot rendered only when non-empty, ~20 tokens measured when populated, byte-identical when not) -- sound, matches established phase idioms exactly (mirrors Phase F's one-lm_complete-per-event discipline). Independently verified: py_compile clean on both files; no debug/exec route present; the one new synchronous lm_complete call (meme mutation) is called from the same tick-thread context as Phase F's birth-persona call, an already-accepted precedent, not a new blocking-risk introduced by this phase. Traced the server process tree after the implementer's restart to confirm it used the canonical visible cmd-window launch (title SimServer) rather than a background task that would die with the subagent's session -- confirmed correct. No process gaps found this time.
